content: Laravel Gate::define() registers authorization callbacks that return true/false. The 'can:' middleware alias (backed by Illuminate\Auth\Middleware\Authorize) is pre-registered in Laravel 12 at config key 'can' => Authorize::class. In routes, Route::middleware(['auth', 'can:admin']) checks auth then gate. Gate::before() can intercept all checks. For TenantSmith, a single Gate::define('central-admin', ...) checking user email against config('auth.central_admin.email') is the simplest approach. Alternatively, a dedicated EnsureCentralAdmin middleware class can check the same and abort(403). The Gate approach is preferred because it integrates with FormRequest::authorize() via ->user()->can('central-admin').
